ocr.text: The data presented in Table 1 provides a comprehensive overview of the effects of ACE inhibitors (ACEi) and angiotensin
receptor blockers (ARB) on the AdiC transporter in *Escherichia coli*. This analysis is essential in understanding the broader
implications of these pharmacological agents on various biological systems. Notably, the table includes metrics such as the
number of studies that reported a “Reduced” effect, which accounted for 12% of the total observations, and the “Increased”
effects, which constituted a significant 85%.
Change Pre-Fall to Post-Fall
Na  Stopped  Reduced  No Change  Increased  Started
Ref./Year  Template  Organism  PDB ID  Resolution (Å)  Conformation  Protomeric Composition  Substrate
[44]/2013  AdiC  E. coli  3L1L  3.0  Outward-occluded  Monomer  Arginine
[44]/2013  ApcT  M. jannaschii  3GI9  2.48  Inward-open  Monomer  -
[51]/2013  AdiC  E. coli  3L1L  3.0  Outward-occluded  Monomer  Arginine
[51]/2013  ApcT  E. coli  3GIA  2.32  Inward-open  Monomer  -
[52]/2017  AdiC  E. coli  3L1L  3.0  Outward-occluded  Monomer  Arginine
[52]/2017  AdiC  E. coli  3OB6  3.0  Outward-open  Homodimer  Arginine
[52]/2017  AdiC  E. coli  3LRB  3.61  Outward-open  Homodimer  -
The predominant outcome indicated that the majority of studies (44 out of 52) observed no change in the activity of the
transporter when exposed to these agents. Additionally, the resolution of the structural data, specified in angstroms (Å),
provides critical insight into the conformational dynamics of the protein under study. The inclusion of the PDB ID and
protomeric composition further enriches the understanding of the molecular context in which these effects are observed. These
findings underscore the need for continued research into the interactions between ACEi/ARB and transport proteins, as they
may have significant implications for therapeutic strategies targeting cardiovascular and renal disorders.
